exemplar.title: To what extent has Shein's unsustainable business practices left a negative social footprint?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Shein's unsustainable business practices left a negative social footprint?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and precise definition of sustainability as the key concept
Consistent linking of analytical tools (TBL, stakeholder matrix, forceâfield) back to sustainability
Extensive use of Sheinâspecific data (hours worked, revenue, emissions) to ground discussion
Strong connection to the organization throughout most of the report
weaknesses: Integration occasionally drifts into general ethics rather than sustainability (e.g., in the stakeholder analysis)
Theoretical frameworks in the introduction could be more explicitly tied to sustainability
Interdependencies among people, planet and profit in the TBL could be explored more deeply
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments showed a strong but not flawless integration of sustainability (band 4) and a fully integrated organizational connection (band 5). Balancing these leads to a 4/5: the report demonstrates clear analytical thinking around sustainability, yet minor lapses prevent a topâband score.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement
All sources are clearly related to labor conditions, emissions and fastâfashion impacts
References list is comprehensive and consistently formatted
Documents are cited effectively in the analysis
weaknesses: Predominant reliance on journalistic rather than peerâreviewed or primary sources
Depth of analysis varies across documents, limiting the range of ideas
No direct stakeholder quotes or primary interviews to enrich authenticity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Quantity and relevance meet high standards (band 4 for number, band 3 for depth). The documents are sufficiently inâdepth but lack the broader range and primary data needed for the top band, warranting a 3/4.

strengths: Appropriate selection of standard tools (TBL, stakeholder analysis, forceâfield) aligned with the research question
Clear definitions and segmentation within frameworks (e.g., profit dimension in TBL)
weaknesses: Limited variety and sophistication of toolsâonly three standard models used
Application sometimes drifts into description without strategic implications
Forceâfield numerical scoring lacks explicit justification or methodological rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both tool selection and application are adequate but not consistently effective (band 3 in strand assessments). To reflect the descriptive tendencies and limited justification, the criterion is scored 2/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of key quantitative data (revenue, emissions, wages) provides an evidence base
Analysis considers social, environmental and economic dimensions
Driving vs. restraining forces are clearly identified in the forceâfield analysis
weaknesses: Analysis remains surfaceâlevel with limited causeâandâeffect exploration
Environmental section is incomplete and cuts off midâanalysis
Data are isolated rather than triangulated, and scoring criteria are not explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analysis are adequate (band 3 each) but lack depth and integration for higher marks. Given the incomplete sections and superficial links, a score of 2/5 accurately reflects âsome analysisâ rather than âmostly effectiveâ.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question in the opening sentence
Findings from each analytical tool are restated and aligned with evidence
Consistent linkage between evidence and conclusions throughout
weaknesses: Conclusion largely reiterates analysis without offering novel insights or strategic recommendations
Some concluding remarks drift into methodological reflection rather than summarizing evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands achieved the highest band (conclusions fully consistent with evidence and explicit answer to the RQ). Despite minor room for deeper insight, the criterion merits the full 3/3.

strengths: Report follows recommended IB structure with clear headings and subheadings
Detailed Table of Contents enhances navigation
Conclusion section is clearly demarcated, aiding reader orientation
weaknesses: Table of Contents lacks page numbers for some entries
Inconsistent styling of section labels (e.g., â2.2â vs. â2.3â)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization and structure are appropriate throughout (band 2). Minor formatting inconsistencies do not impede structure, so the full 2/2 is justified.

strengths: All required presentation elements are included (title, RQ, word count, TOC, citations, references, appendix)
Research question and word count are prominently displayed
Captions for figures are accurate and informative
weaknesses: Hyperlink styling in the âOther resourcesâ section is inconsistent
Minor variations in formatting across appendices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets all IB presentation requirements without omissions (band 2). Formatting inconsistencies are cosmetic and do not affect compliance, so the full 2/2 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows IB-recommended sections (introduction, analysis, conclusion, references, appendix) and uses clear headings and sub-headings, demonstrating appropriate organisation throughout.

The five sources are all clearly related to labour conditions, emissions and fast-fashion impacts, and they are cited in the analysis.  However, depth is uneven: most figures come from journalism rather than industry reports, and no primary data beyond corporate reports are supplied.  This meets the rubric for ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ but not the broader ârange of ideasâ expected for the top band.
All required elementsâtitle, RQ, word count, TOC, citations, reference list, appendixâare present, placing the submission in the highest presentation band.
Exactly five supporting documents (SD1-SD5) are identified, satisfying the required 3-5 range without exceeding it, so the highest band for quantity is appropriate.

Each tool is applied to Shein with supporting evidence, yet the application sometimes drifts into description (e.g., power-interest grid lacks discussion of strategic implications) and numerical scoring in the force-field is asserted without justification.  Relevance is clear but effectiveness is only adequate, fitting the 3-mark band.

Key quantitative data (revenue, valuation, emissions, wages, hours) are selected and referenced, showing an adequate evidence base.  The dataset is not extensiveâfigures are isolated and not triangulatedâso âadequateâ rather than âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ is the best match.

Analysis goes beyond narration by weighing social, environmental and economic outcomes, and by comparing driving vs. restraining forces.  Nonetheless, much commentary remains surface-level; cause-and-effect links are asserted rather than rigorously examined.  This corresponds to âsome analysisâ (band 3) rather than âmostly effectiveâ (band 4).
The research question is explicitly answered in the opening sentence of the conclusion and supported by a brief rationale, satisfying the highest band for directly addressing the RQ.

Three standard tools (TBL, stakeholder analysis, force-field) are chosen.  They are appropriate to the RQ but represent an adequate rather than especially varied or sophisticated toolkit; therefore the middle band for selection is warranted.
The conclusion restates the findings from each tool and aligns with the evidence presented, fulfilling the descriptor for full consistency with evidence (top band within this criterion).

All sections focus squarely on Shein.  Primary and secondary data, figures, and examples (hours worked, revenue, emissions, valuation, product turnover, etc.) are continuously employed to illustrate points about the organisation.  Every analytical tool is populated with Shein-specific evidence, demonstrating a comprehensive and fully integrated connection to the organisation that aligns with the top-band descriptor.

The student defines sustainability and keeps the discussion framed around the social-, environmental- and economic-dimensions of the concept.  Each analytical tool (TBL, stakeholder matrix, force-field) is explicitly linked back to sustainability or the firmâs âsocial footprint,â showing a strong but not entirely seamless integration.  In a few places (e.g., Stakeholder Analysis) the link reverts to general ethics rather than sustainability per se, so the integration is substantial but not flawlessly woven throughout, matching the descriptor for a 4-mark band rather than the fully embedded approach required for 5.

annotations.comment: Research question is clearly and prominently displayed, enhancing focus and coherence of the assessment.
Key concept âSustainabilityâ is identified, but further integration with theoretical frameworks in the introduction would strengthen its application.
Word count is clearly stated, meeting IB requirements and demonstrating attention to presentation guidelines.
Effective inclusion of a detailed Table of Contents reflects strong organization and aids navigation.
Entries in the Table of Contents lack page numbers; ensure each section is linked to its corresponding page for clarity.
Formatting of section labels (e.g., â2.2â vs â2.3â) is inconsistent; apply uniform styling to improve structural coherence.
Introduction situates Sheinâs global reach effectively but would benefit from explicitly stating why social footprint is the focal lens.
Strength: Clear definition of sustainability provides a solid theoretical anchor for the analysis.
Use of Shein-specific data (valuation, hours worked) grounds the introduction in context and strengthens the connection to the organization.
Weakness: Sentence truncation (âThis hasâ) prevents clear explanation of methodological approach; complete your tool-selection rationale.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good choice of the Triple Bottom Line framework, aligning appropriately with the key concept of sustainability.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider citing a more recent sustainability framework to contextualize the relevance of TBL in the fast fashion industry.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Strong use of SD2 data to illustrate worker conditions; deepen the link to brand reputation by analyzing consumer responses.
Weakness: Environmental section is incomplete and cuts off mid-sentence; include full data and analysis of emissions and waste.
Incorporates competitor benchmarks, but lacks a defined comparative framework; consider adding metrics to measure Shein against peers.
Effective segmentation of the profit dimension clarifies economic impact within the Triple Bottom Line framework.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Profit figures are well presented; enhance analysis by discussing multi-year trends to demonstrate sustainability of earnings.
TBL summary integrates people, planet, and profit effectively but should explore interdependencies for deeper insight.
Definition of stakeholder analysis is clear; specify how this tool aligns with assessing sustainability outcomes.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Stakeholder matrix is well formatted; enrich by naming specific entities in each quadrant rather than generic group labels.
Group categorization aligns with the power-interest grid, but thresholds for âlowâ vs âhighâ power/interest need justification.
Group B analysis insightfully addresses customer power; further depth could be achieved by including customer sentiment metrics.
Stakeholder narratives are insightful; incorporating direct stakeholder quotes would increase authenticity and depth.
Force Field Analysis method is described well; clarify the rationale for using a 1â5 scale to support scoring validity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Force field table presents driving and restraining forces clearly, but numerical ratings lack explicit scoring criteria.
Caption accurately describes Fig. 2, aiding reader comprehension of the force-field analysis.
Analysis contrasts driving and restraining forces effectively; further discussion on strategic recommendations would enhance evaluation.
Conclusion section is clearly demarcated with a heading, strengthening overall structure and reader orientation.
Conclusion synthesizes key findings but largely reiterates analysis; include recommendations or novel insights to meet highest band.
Additional concluding remarks veer into methodological reflection rather than summarizing evidence; maintain focus on key conclusions.
Proposed areas for further investigation are thoughtful; specifying research methods would make recommendations more actionable.
References list for supporting documents is comprehensive and consistently formatted, demonstrating strong sourcing.
Predominance of secondary journalistic sources limits academic depth; consider integrating primary data or peer-reviewed studies.
Other resources section enhances context, but hyperlink styling varies; standardize URL presentation for consistency.
Consistent reference formatting across all sources supports academic integrity and professional presentation.
Inclusion of UN SDG graphic enriches visual context but integration with main analysis is minimal; reference specific goals in the text.
